source_tiers.condition: No DEM file or direct DEM URL was provided, and the user specifies a particular US dataset or source name.
policy: Search DAV for the requested dataset, including DEM and lidar candidates.
If the dataset is accessible and downloadable, download it even when full-dataset acquisition is required.
Before a large full-dataset download, inform the user of the estimated size and likely wait time.
If the downloaded product is not already a raster DEM, preserve the source files and metadata but do not claim a CELERIS bathymetry artifact.

source_tiers.condition: No specific dataset was provided, the requested dataset was not available, or the requested dataset was not downloadable.
policy: Try USGS CoNED WCS extraction first for US coastal AOIs.
If CoNED WCS has no coverage or cannot produce a GeoTIFF subset, try NOAA Sea Level Rise Viewer DEM.
Use native or service-native resolution and datum, and record which one was used in metadata.

source_tiers.condition: Tier 1 and Tier 2 cannot produce a usable source DEM, or the user explicitly requests public NOAA gridded sources such as NOAA DEM Global Mosaic, CUDEM, CRM, or ETOPO.
policy: Query NOAA DEM Global Mosaic for the best available gridded raster over the AOI, then fall back to CRM Mosaic and ETOPO 2022 Bedrock 15 arcseconds.
Export a clipped GeoTIFF for the requested AOI through NOAA Grid Extract ImageServer endpoints.
Preserve service-native resolution, datum, source bbox, and request bbox in DEM metadata.

nodes.description: Extract location, AOI, resolution, datum, file hints, and desired products from a chat instruction.
Inspect chat attachments, expand ZIP bundles, and rank supported DEM-like files.
Read GeoTIFF rasters with rasterio/GDAL metadata.
Read DEM-like 2D variables from NetCDF datasets.
Read ESRI ASCII grid files.
Read 2D numeric matrices or regular XYZ point grids.
Read 2D numeric DEM arrays from MATLAB files.
Apply sign, unit, CRS, datum, nodata, and downsampling options.
Check shape, nodata, CRS, datum, spacing, sign convention risks, and elevation ranges.
Write celeris_bathy.mat, preview.png, and dem_manifest.json. bathy.txt is deferred to the config-generation node.
Apply the tiered online source hierarchy: user-specified US dataset through DAV, then CoNED WCS, then NOAA SLR DEM, then public NOAA gridded sources.
Route source-based DEM requests through Tier 1 user-specified DAV datasets, Tier 2 default US DEM sources, and Tier 3 public NOAA gridded sources.
Resolve a named center point to lon/lat using request coordinates, curated aliases, or a geocoder fallback.
For coastal center-plus-domain AOIs, use local OSM coastline first and Natural Earth fallback to snap the resolved center to the nearest shoreline before building the DEM bbox.
Create a WGS84 bounding box and DAV WKT polygon from center lon/lat and requested domain size.
Call the NOAA Digital Coast Data Access Viewer /search/missions endpoint for Lidar and DEM candidates intersecting the AOI.
Tier 1 DAV search for a user-specified US dataset, including DEM and lidar candidates intersecting the AOI.
Match a user-specified dataset name against DAV candidates and choose the best downloadable candidate before falling back to default DEM sources.
Rank NOAA DAV candidates by data type, datum match, target resolution, recency, direct export support, and bulk download availability.
For NOAA DAV candidates with an ArcGIS ImageServer, export a GeoTIFF for the requested AOI and grid size.

Download a user-specified DAV dataset through direct full-product or provider links when AOI extraction is unavailable; estimate and report size before large downloads. Bulk tile indexing remains separate.
Match a US coastal AOI against a cached CoNED Project Viewer layer catalog and choose the intersecting WCS coverage.
Call GeoServer WCS DescribeCoverage for the selected CoNED coverage to capture CRS, bounds, grid spacing, native format, and service-native resolution.
Call GeoServer WCS GetCoverage to export a GeoTIFF subset for the AOI, then route it through DEM standardization.
Search for NOAA Sea Level Rise Viewer DEM coverage after CoNED is unavailable or cannot extract the AOI.
Export the NOAA Sea Level Rise Viewer DEM for the AOI in its native datum and resolution, then route it through DEM standardization.
Create public NOAA gridded candidates for the AOI, including NOAA DEM Global Mosaic best-available raster, CRM Mosaic, and ETOPO 2022 Bedrock 15 arcseconds.
Prefer user-requested CRM or ETOPO when specified; otherwise try NOAA DEM Global Mosaic first, then CRM and ETOPO fallbacks.
Export a clipped GeoTIFF from a public NOAA Grid Extract ArcGIS ImageServer dataset and route it through DEM standardization.
Download a user-provided DEM URL and route it through the normal attachment workflow.
Ask the LLM to merge user-provided simulation setup details with CELERIS defaults and return a complete config request object.
Validate first-pass CELERIS config requirements, including the required wave direction and exactly one incoming wave boundary.
Load outputs/celeris_bathy.mat and support z/h bathymetry variables plus x/y coordinates.
Interpolate canonical bathymetry to the requested CELERIS dx/dy model grid.
Write CELERIS bathy.txt from the interpolated model grid.
Generate waves.txt using a Python port of the Korea JLOTS spectrum_2D_periodic.m workflow.
Write config.json using the Korea JLOTS Step2 defaults plus LLM/user-provided overrides and derived grid fields.
Write celeris_case_manifest.json with config, wave, grid, provenance, and validation metadata.

After CELERIS inputs are generated, resolve the final model-domain bbox, retrieve tiled satellite imagery from Esri World Imagery with EOX Sentinel-2 cloudless fallback, and write overlay.jpg plus overlay_manifest.json with a maximum side length of 8192 pixels.

Queue a whitelisted semantic runtime command for the embedded root CELERIS agent runner. Implemented commands include examples, pause/resume, Modify Visualization controls, and Add Engineered Design Components controls listed in celeris_runtime_controls.json.
